name: b3_slice_isolation_twocontroller_smoke_T1_atlanta
translation_honesty: This B3 smoke benchmark models slice isolation as a symbolic flowspace boundary. Slice A and Slice B have separate logical controllers, distinct ingress classes, and distinct endpoints. The Slice B endpoint is legitimate for Slice B traffic but forbidden for Slice A traffic. The faulty model gives CSliceA an overbroad rule that sends Slice A traffic to the Slice B endpoint. The control model confines each slice to its own endpoint.

embedding.meaning: B3 checks slice isolation with two logical slice controllers. CSliceA controls Slice A policy and CSliceB controls Slice B policy. The control model sends Slice A traffic to the Slice A endpoint and Slice B traffic to the Slice B endpoint. The faulty model gives Slice A an overbroad policy that sends it to the Slice B endpoint.
